title: Investment Basics
description: Test your knowledge of basic investment concepts

questions.question: What is the minimum lock-in period for PPF?
questions.explanation: PPF has a 15-year lock-in period, making it suitable for long-term wealth building.

questions.question: Which investment option offers tax deduction under Section 80C?
questions.explanation: PPF offers tax deduction under Section 80C up to ₹1.5 lakh annually.

questions.question: What does SIP stand for?
questions.explanation: SIP stands for Systematic Investment Plan - investing regularly in mutual funds.

title: Credit & Banking
description: Understanding credit scores and banking concepts

questions.question: What is the ideal credit utilization ratio?
questions.explanation: Credit utilization should be below 30% for a good credit score.

questions.question: What does CIBIL score range from?
questions.explanation: CIBIL score ranges from 300 to 900, with higher scores indicating better creditworthiness.

questions.question: What is the maximum DTI ratio recommended by RBI?
questions.explanation: RBI recommends keeping DTI ratio below 40% for loan approval.

questions.question: Which bank offers the lowest home loan rates?
questions.explanation: SBI typically offers the lowest home loan rates starting from 8.4%.

questions.question: What is the maximum deduction under Section 80C?
questions.explanation: Section 80C allows maximum deduction of ₹1.5 lakh annually.

questions.question: Which investment has the shortest lock-in period for tax benefits?
questions.explanation: ELSS has only 3-year lock-in period, shortest among tax-saving options.

questions.question: What is the HRA exemption limit for metro cities?
questions.explanation: HRA exemption is 50% of basic salary for metro cities (Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata).

questions.question: Which of these is NOT eligible for Section 80C deduction?
questions.explanation: Regular mutual funds are not eligible for Section 80C deduction, only ELSS funds are.

questions.question: What is the additional deduction available under Section 80CCD(1B)?
questions.explanation: Section 80CCD(1B) provides additional deduction of ₹50,000 for NPS contributions.
